name: Chicken Paprikas
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil, up to 4 tablespoons
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the chicken with salt and pepper.
2. Heat oil in a 4- or 6-quart Presto pressure cooker.
3. Saute onions, green pepper, and garlic until tender; remove from the pan.
4. Brown the chicken a few pieces at a time; set aside.
5. Add tomato sauce, paprika, dill weed, and a small amount of chicken broth to the oil in the pressure cooker; stir until smooth.
6. Add remaining broth, stirring to mix.
7. Return the chicken and vegetables to the pressure cooker.
8. Close the pressure cooker cover securely.
9. Place the pressure regulator on the vent pipe.
10. Cook for 8 minutes at 15 pounds pressure with the regulator rocking slowly.
11. Remove the pressure cooker from heat and carefully release the pressure.
12. Cool the pressure cooker.
13. Transfer the chicken and vegetables to a warm dish.
14. Stir flour into sour cream; add to the hot liquid in the pressure cooker.
15. Cook and stir until the mixture simmers and thickens.
16. Pour the sauce over the chicken.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
